commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs maintainer decision on the per-gateway endpoint contract plus contributor-owned live behavior proof; this is not an automation-safe repair lane while the storage direction is unsettled.; Needs attention: The global endpoint override can direct the active gateway shared token to a stale or wrong local browser-control listener after gateway switches.; Review finding: [P1] Scope BrowserControlPort to the a... (sha=4f83e876ff1d7f3938da1fa166adcfa5e9b3f8da)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a settings.json-editable BrowserControlPort override, wires it into BrowserProxyCapability construction, and adds handler tests for configured-port routing and invalid-port errors.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows the override is global while browser.proxy sends the active shared gateway token to the configured endpoint. The underlying split/remote success path was not live-proven by the PR artifacts.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer decision on the per-gateway endpoint contract plus contributor-owned live behavior proof; this is not an automation-safe repair lane while the storage direction is unsettled.

target.title: browser.proxy: allow overriding the browser-control port for split/remote gateways
target.body:
`BrowserProxyCapability` hard-derives the browser-control host as `gatewayPort + 2` on `127.0.0.1`. That is correct for a co-located gateway, but breaks for a **split/remote gateway** тАФ e.g. the gateway running in WSL2 with the Companion on the Windows host, where the browser-control host is reached on a different local port (an SSH-forwarded port). Every `browser.proxy` request then fails as unreachable at the derived port.

This adds one optional override тАФ `BrowserControlPort` тАФ so the Companion can target the actual forwarded browser-control port. **Default is unchanged**: when unset, the port is derived exactly as today (`gatewayPort + 2`).

The browser-control **auth token is unchanged** тАФ it continues to come from the per-gateway shared token (`record.SharedGatewayToken`) via the existing credential path, so this PR adds **no new credential storage** and stays within the `GatewayRegistry` credential model.

## Changes
- `OpenClaw.Shared/SettingsData.cs` тАФ add `BrowserControlPort` (int?). Null (default) = derive as today.
- `OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI/Services/SettingsManager.cs` тАФ forwarder.
- `OpenClaw.Shared/Capabilities/BrowserProxyCapability.cs` тАФ ctor gains `controlPortOverride`; `TryResolveControlEndpoint` honours an explicit port (TCP-range validated) else `gatewayPort + 2`.
- `OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI/Services/NodeService.cs` тАФ pass the setting into the capability ctor.
- `tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/CapabilityTests.cs` тАФ +2 `[Fact]`: port override targets the configured port; out-of-range port errors cleanly.

## Scope boundary
- Port-only: a port is not a credential, so this stays clear of the `GatewayRegistry` credential boundary.
- Settings-editable; no Settings UI added (advanced/optional override).
- No gateway-side change; Companion-only.

## Tested
- `dotnet test tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests` -> 2267 passed, 0 failed, 29 skipped (incl. the 2 new port facts).
- `dotnet build src/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI -c Debug -r win-x64` -> all assemblies compile (the wiring builds).

## Not tested
- No Settings UI, so no UI test.
- Live split-gateway `browser.proxy` round-trip is not yet in CI тАФ the unit tests cover port resolution; the motivating repro was a WSL2 <-> Windows packet capture showing the request failing to reach the host at the derived port.

## Risk / mitigation
- *Misconfigured port* -> out-of-range is validated and returns a clear error; unset falls back to today's `gatewayPort + 2`.
- *No credential change* -> the auth-token path is untouched (per-gateway shared token), so there is no new secret storage or gateway-switch credential concern.

---
*Supersedes the initial revision, which also added a global `BrowserControlToken` setting. Dropped after review: current source already sends the per-gateway shared token (`record.SharedGatewayToken`) to the browser-control host (`NodeService` comment + ctor), so the token override was redundant тАФ the only genuine gap was the hard-coded control port.*
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
